title: oncoba macrophylla
intro: Oncoba macrophylla, also known as the nettle tree, is a tropical plant native to Africa, belonging to the family Salicaceae. Traditionally, Oncoba macrophylla has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Oncoba macrophylla is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and the leaves and bark are used in herbal preparations, including teas and poultices, to harness its medicinal properties.
